metadata_checks.comments: Title, year, and paper type are correct. DOI is absent as expected. Core scope is a Swiss referendum on fossil/non-renewable energy taxes, with subsidy proposals as secondary context.

determinant_checks.comment: Supported; a main positive predictor of approval and turnout for the energy-tax proposals. Substantive determinant, though also a model control.
Supported; higher education consistently increased approval and participation for the energy-tax proposals. Keep as a substantive demographic control.
Supported; age 60+ reduced support, especially for the green tax reform and solar initiative. Clear negative demographic effect.
Supported; more cars lowered support, especially for the green tax reform. Direct exposure-to-fuel-tax burden mechanism.
Supported; residents of larger municipalities were more favorable in the tax votes. Geographic control with a positive association.
Mainly concerns the energy conservation package, a subsidy counter-proposal, not the carbon/fuel-tax votes. Exclude from the strict tax corpus.
determinant_checks.suggested_correction: Exclude or relabel as a non-tax subsidy-proposal determinant.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Priority government intervention vs. competition (1 = competition, 0 = regulation)
determinant_checks.comment: Supported; pro-regulation respondents were more favorable to the energy taxes. Substantive ideology determinant.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Concern with income inequality (1 = at least some, 0 = no or little concern)
determinant_checks.comment: Supported; greater inequality concern lowered approval of the energy-tax proposals, especially the green tax reform.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Concern with unemployment (1 = at least some, 0 = no or little concern)
determinant_checks.comment: Supported but weak; the negative effect is reported, though the author treats it as fragile and not very convincing.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Higher tax rate / much higher tax rate of the green tax reform
determinant_checks.comment: Supported as a design feature; higher rates increased rejection among exposed groups, but the effect was not decisive for most voters.

determinant_checks.raw_name_in_paper: Mode of revenue recycling / subsidies for renewable energy and energy conservation
determinant_checks.comment: Combines the green tax reform's payroll recycling with subsidy-based proposals. The subsidy-based part is not a direct tax determinant.
determinant_checks.suggested_correction: Split payroll recycling for the green tax reform from the subsidy proposals; drop the non-tax part.

missing_tax_determinants.outcome_variable: approval of the energy-tax proposals / green tax reform
missing_tax_determinants.evidence_quote: Household income is not a clear-cut determinant of the acceptance of green taxes among those who participated in the vote... estimated negative coefficients suggest...
missing_tax_determinants.comment: Discussed as a determinant despite weak significance; negative sign is interpretable in the vote equations.

final_recommendation_summary: Keep the paper in the strict tax corpus. Metadata are correct. The extraction needs cleanup: remove or separate subsidy-proposal determinants (especially language and earmarked subsidies) and retain only the tax-relevant recycling item for the green tax reform. Household income should be added as a secondary direct determinant.
